tools.name: alarmModels.createAlarmModel
tools.description: Creates an alarm model to monitor an AWS IoT Events input attribute. You can use the alarm to get notified when the value is outside a specified range. For more information, see Create an alarm model in the AWS IoT Events Developer Guide.

tools.name: alarmModels.deleteAlarmModel
tools.description: Deletes an alarm model. Any alarm instances that were created based on this alarm model are also deleted. This action can't be undone.

tools.name: alarmModels.describeAlarmModel
tools.description: Retrieves information about an alarm model. If you don't specify a value for the alarmModelVersion parameter, the latest version is returned.

tools.name: alarmModels.listAlarmModels
tools.description: Lists the alarm models that you created. The operation returns only the metadata associated with each alarm model.

tools.name: alarmModels.listAlarmModelVersions
tools.description: Lists all the versions of an alarm model. The operation returns only the metadata associated with each alarm model version.

tools.name: alarmModels.updateAlarmModel
tools.description: Updates an alarm model. Any alarms that were created based on the previous version are deleted and then created again as new data arrives.

tools.name: analysis.describeDetectorModelAnalysis
tools.description:
Retrieves runtime information about a detector model analysis.

After AWS IoT Events starts analyzing your detector model, you have up to 24 hours to retrieve the analysis results.

tools.name: analysis.getDetectorModelAnalysisResults
tools.description:
Retrieves one or more analysis results of the detector model.

After AWS IoT Events starts analyzing your detector model, you have up to 24 hours to retrieve the analysis results.

tools.name: analysis.startDetectorModelAnalysis
tools.description: Performs an analysis of your detector model. For more information, see Troubleshooting a detector model in the AWS IoT Events Developer Guide.

tools.name: detectorModels.createDetectorModel
tools.description: Creates a detector model.

tools.name: detectorModels.deleteDetectorModel
tools.description: Deletes a detector model. Any active instances of the detector model are also deleted.

tools.name: detectorModels.describeDetectorModel
tools.description: Describes a detector model. If the version parameter is not specified, information about the latest version is returned.

tools.name: detectorModels.listDetectorModels
tools.description: Lists the detector models you have created. Only the metadata associated with each detector model is returned.

tools.name: detectorModels.listDetectorModelVersions
tools.description: Lists all the versions of a detector model. Only the metadata associated with each detector model version is returned.

tools.name: detectorModels.updateDetectorModel
tools.description: Updates a detector model. Detectors (instances) spawned by the previous version are deleted and then re-created as new inputs arrive.

tools.name: inputRoutings.listInputRoutings
tools.description: Lists one or more input routings.

tools.name: inputs.createInput
tools.description: Creates an input.

tools.name: inputs.deleteInput
tools.description: Deletes an input.

tools.name: inputs.describeInput
tools.description: Describes an input.

tools.name: inputs.listInputs
tools.description: Lists the inputs you have created.

tools.name: inputs.updateInput
tools.description: Updates an input.

tools.name: logging.describeLoggingOptions
tools.description: Retrieves the current settings of the AWS IoT Events logging options.

tools.name: logging.putLoggingOptions
tools.description:
Sets or updates the AWS IoT Events logging options.

If you update the value of any loggingOptions field, it takes up to one minute for the change to take effect. If you change the policy attached to the role you specified in the roleArn field (for example, to correct an invalid policy), it takes up to five minutes for that change to take effect.

tools.name: tagsResourceArn.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists the tags (metadata) you have assigned to the resource.

tools.name: tagsResourceArn.tagResource
tools.description: Adds to or modifies the tags of the given resource. Tags are metadata that can be used to manage a resource.

tools.name: tagsResourceArnTagKeys.untagResource
tools.description: Removes the given tags (metadata) from the resource.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
